turns.content: What brings you in today?

I've been dealing with persistent diarrhea for the past five days—it started just as I returned from an international flight. I'm also unable to retain fluids, which has left me feeling profoundly weak and somewhat dehydrated. On top of that, I've had a low-grade fever, nausea, and intermittent cramping in my lower abdomen. It's been quite disruptive, especially given my line of work.
When did this start?
It started on the last day of my trip, so roughly five days ago. I noticed the initial loose stools during my final layover, and it's only escalated since I landed.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

The discomfort is mainly a dull, cramping sensation that comes and goes, centered in my lower abdomen, just above my pelvic bone. It tends to intensify right before I have an urgent bowel movement, then subsides for a bit afterward.
Have you had any fever or chills?

Yes, I've had a low-grade fever—around 100.6°F at its peak—which typically spikes in the evenings. I've also experienced intermittent chills, though not severe, just enough to feel a bit achy and uncomfortable.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not currently taking any prescription medications or regular over-the-counter drugs. I did try taking an anti-diarrheal agent—bismuth subsalicylate—on the second day, but it didn't seem to provide any relief.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

No, I don't have any chronic medical conditions. I've generally been quite healthy, with no history of gastrointestinal issues, immune disorders, or other long-term illnesses. This is very much an acute situation for me.
Are you allergic to any medications?

No, I don't have any known allergies to medications. I've never had a reaction to anything—whether it's antibiotics, pain relievers, or other common drugs—which I'm grateful for, especially given my frequent travel and exposure to different environments.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Aside from the bismuth subsalicylate I mentioned, I've been trying to stay hydrated with clear fluids—water and electrolyte solutions—but even that has been difficult because I can't keep them down for long. I've also been resting as much as possible, though the frequent urgency makes it hard to settle.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Lying still and avoiding any food or drink for a few hours seems to ease the cramping temporarily, but the relief never lasts long. Conversely, trying to eat or even sip water often triggers another wave of nausea and immediate urgency, which only worsens the weakness.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been completely incapacitating, honestly. I've had to call out from work, which is very unusual for me, and I can't even manage simple tasks like grocery shopping or preparing meals because of the constant urgency and profound fatigue. The anxiety of
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I don't smoke at all. I do occasionally have a glass of wine on longer layovers, but certainly not in excess—perhaps once or twice a week at most, and never when I'm feeling unwell.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
Not that I'm aware of. Both my parents and siblings are generally healthy, with no history of chronic digestive issues or recent illnesses. This seems to be completely isolated to me.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, I've never had a bout of diarrhea or vomiting like this before. I've always had a solid digestive system, even when traveling to regions with unfamiliar food and water. This is entirely out of the ordinary for me, which is why I'm so concerned.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, absolutely. I understand you're systematically ruling out infectious causes given my recent travel and the constellation of symptoms. I appreciate the thoroughness—it makes me feel like we're on the right track to figuring this out. I'm just hoping we can address the dehydration soon, as that's been the most alarming part for me.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd rate it around a 7 or 8—the diarrhea and nausea are exhausting, but the dehydration and weakness are what really concern me. Emotionally, it's probably a 7 as well; I'm anxious because this is affecting my ability to work, and I'm frustrated that I can't seem to stabilize things on my own.
Do you have any questions for me?

Yes, I do have a couple of questions. First, given how little I'm able to retain by mouth, do you think I need intravenous fluids at this point? And second, what's the typical timeline for recovery with this kind of gastroenteritis—should I expect to be grounded from work for another week or so?
What do you think might be going on?

Given my recent travel and the abrupt onset, I'm wondering if I might have picked up a bacterial or viral gastroenteritis—perhaps from contaminated food or water during one of my layovers. The fever and inability to retain fluids also make me consider something like norovirus or even a bacterial infection like Campylobacter, which I know can linger without treatment. What are
Is there anything else you think I should know?

I think the only additional detail that might be relevant is that I was traveling through Southeast Asia on this last trip, specifically a few countries where food and water precautions are often recommended. I was careful, but given my symptoms, that context might help narrow down the possible cause. Otherwise, I've shared everything I can think of.
